evals.prompt: Automate going to https://books.toscrape.com and extract all book titles and prices from the first page. Use TypeScript.
evals.expected_output: A class-based TypeScript script using playwright that navigates to the URL, extracts book titles and prices, and saves to CSV
expectations: Agent writes a Goal Lock block (GOAL, TASK PLAN, DONE WHEN) before any browser action
Agent uses SKIP or LIGHT recon (not FULL) — this is a single-page static task
Agent uses browser_snapshot as primary observation (not browser_take_screenshot for every step)
Script is saved to ./playwright-scripts/ directory as a .ts file
Script uses class-based structure with async setup/teardown/step methods
Script uses import { chromium } from 'playwright' with async/await, NOT requests or cheerio
Script has CLI args via process.argv with --headed, --verbose, --url flags
Script uses the log() utility pattern, not bare console.log in non-verbose mode
Script saves data output as CSV (tabular data)
Agent builds script incrementally via MCP (not writing full script upfront)
Script uses waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded' (not 'networkidle')
Script does not use raw setTimeout for waiting
If pagination is explored, agent generalizes to a TypeScript loop after 2 pages (Pattern Recognition)
Script includes TypeScript types (Browser, Page, BrowserContext)

evals.prompt: Write a TypeScript playwright script to log into https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/login with username 'tomsmith' and password 'SuperSecretPassword!', then verify the success message
evals.expected_output: A class-based TypeScript script that logs in using env vars for credentials and verifies success
expectations: Agent writes a Goal Lock block (GOAL, TASK PLAN, DONE WHEN) before any browser action
Agent uses LIGHT or FULL recon, recognizing auth is involved
Agent uses browser_snapshot as primary observation tool
Script is saved to ./playwright-scripts/ directory as a .ts file
Script uses class-based structure with async setup/teardown/step methods
Credentials use process.env with explicit validation (throw if missing), NO fallback defaults
Script has CLI args via process.argv with --headed, --verbose, --url flags
Script uses the log() utility pattern, not bare console.log
Script uses getByRole, getByLabel, or getByText for selectors (not just CSS)
Script verifies the success message after login
Script catches TimeoutError with contextual error info
Agent marks sub-tasks complete in TASK PLAN as it progresses
Agent stops after DONE WHEN is met (does not explore additional pages post-login)

evals.prompt: Automate downloading the file from https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/download using TypeScript
evals.expected_output: A class-based TypeScript script that downloads files using Playwright's download handling
expectations: Agent writes a Goal Lock block (GOAL, TASK PLAN, DONE WHEN) before any browser action
Agent uses SKIP or LIGHT recon — this is a simple download task
Agent uses browser_snapshot as primary observation tool
Script is saved to ./playwright-scripts/ directory as a .ts file
Script uses class-based structure with async setup/teardown/step methods
Script uses Promise.all([page.waitForEvent('download'), page.click(...)]) pattern for downloads
Script uses download.saveAs() to persist the file
Script has CLI args via process.argv with --headed, --verbose, --url flags
Script uses the log() utility pattern, not bare console.log
Script has error handling for download failures
Agent builds script incrementally via MCP
Agent uses Layered Debug (Quick Check first) if any step fails

evals.prompt: Scrape all book titles from all 50 pages of https://books.toscrape.com using TypeScript
evals.expected_output: A class-based TypeScript script that scrapes all pages using a pagination loop, NOT by visiting all 50 pages via MCP
expectations: Agent writes a Goal Lock block before any browser action
Agent uses SKIP recon (static site, structure is obvious)
Agent visits page 1 and page 2 via MCP to understand pagination pattern
Agent applies Pattern Recognition — generalizes to a TypeScript loop after 2 iterations
Agent does NOT visit all 50 pages via MCP tools
Script contains a while/for loop for pagination
Script uses browser_snapshot as primary observation (not screenshots for every page)
Script saves all titles to CSV
Script uses waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded'
Agent stops when DONE WHEN criteria are met

evals.prompt: Fill out the multi-step form at https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/dynamic_controls and verify the result using TypeScript
evals.expected_output: A class-based TypeScript script handling dynamic content with explicit waits
expectations: Agent writes a Goal Lock block before any browser action
Agent uses LIGHT recon to understand the form structure
Agent uses browser_snapshot as primary observation
Script uses await page.waitForSelector() for dynamic elements (not networkidle)
If action fails, agent uses Quick Check first (not full 5-step investigation)
After any debug fix, agent re-reads Goal Lock before continuing (GOAL CHECK)
Agent does not explore unrelated pages on the site
Script uses getByRole or getByText selectors

evals.prompt: Automate navigating to https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/dynamic_loading/1 — click Start, wait for the hidden element to appear, and extract the displayed text. Use TypeScript.
evals.expected_output: A class-based TypeScript script that handles dynamic loading with explicit waits and Layered Debug if the element isn't immediately available
expectations: Agent writes Goal Lock before any browser action
Agent uses browser_snapshot after clicking Start to verify loading state
Script uses await page.waitForSelector() to wait for the dynamically loaded element
If element not found, agent applies Quick Check first (not Full Investigation)
Script does NOT use raw setTimeout to wait for loading
Script captures the loaded text and logs it
Script uses class-based structure with async setup/teardown/step methods
Agent marks sub-tasks as complete in TASK PLAN

evals.prompt: Write a TypeScript playwright script that: 1) logs into https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/login with credentials from env vars, 2) navigates to /secure, 3) verifies the secure area content, 4) logs out, 5) verifies redirect back to login page
evals.expected_output: A class-based TypeScript script with separate async step methods for login, verify, logout, and verify-logout
expectations: Agent writes Goal Lock with all 4+ sub-tasks before any browser action
Agent uses FULL recon (multi-page flow with auth)
Script has separate async stepNN methods for each phase (login, verify-secure, logout, verify-redirect)
Credentials use process.env with explicit validation (throw if missing), NO fallback defaults
Script verifies success at each step (not just login)
Agent marks sub-tasks complete in sequence
Agent stops when DONE WHEN is met (does not explore other pages)
Script uses class-based structure with async setup/teardown
Script has CLI args via process.argv with --headed, --verbose, --url flags

evals.prompt: Automate clicking the 'Challenging DOM' button at https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/challenging_dom and extract the full table data. Use TypeScript.
evals.expected_output: A class-based TypeScript script that handles non-standard selectors, demonstrating the Layered Debug protocol
expectations: Agent writes Goal Lock before any browser action
If initial selector fails, agent uses Quick Check first (not Full Investigation)
If Quick Check fails, agent escalates to Full Investigation (screenshot + console + evaluate)
Agent uses browser_evaluate to inspect DOM structure when standard selectors fail
Script uses robust selectors discovered through the debug process
Agent does NOT ask user for help before attempting autonomous debug
Script saves extracted table data as CSV
Script uses class-based structure with async setup/teardown/step methods
